name: Salesforce
notes: Free Developer Edition org gives full API access; production orgs need a paid plan.

name: HubSpot
notes: Free developer test account + free CRM tier. Ships an official MCP server.

name: Pipedrive
notes: Free 14-30 day trial gives an API token immediately.

name: Attio
notes: Free plan issues API keys; has a first-party MCP server.

name: Twenty
notes: Fully open source; self-host or use cloud free tier, API key from account settings.

name: Podio
blocker: Dated API, sparse docs, uncertain long-term support
notes: Free developer account, app-level OAuth client needed.

name: Zoho CRM
what: CRM part of Zoho One suite
notes: Free tier + free developer sandbox; standard OAuth2 with scopes.

name: Close
notes: Free trial issues API keys instantly.

name: Copper
blocker: Trial required to obtain API key; smaller API surface
notes: Free trial account gets an API key from settings.

name: DealCloud
blocker: Enterprise-only, requires existing client relationship for API access
notes: No self-serve signup; API access provisioned per client contract.

name: Zendesk
notes: Free trial gives API token immediately.

name: Intercom
notes: Free developer workspace; ships an official MCP server.

name: Freshdesk
notes: Free trial account gets an API key from profile settings.

name: Front
notes: Free trial; API tokens generated per-account or via OAuth app.

name: Pylon
what: B2B support platform for Slack-first companies
blocker: Newer/smaller API surface, fewer public examples
notes: Paid plans required to unlock full API; still self-serve signup.

name: LiveAgent
notes: Free trial provides API key from account.

name: Plain
notes: GraphQL-only API; free tier for small teams.

name: Help Scout
notes: Free trial; OAuth2 client credentials app flow.

name: Gorgias
notes: Free trial account; app review needed to publish a public OAuth integration.

name: Gladly
blocker: Enterprise-only sales-led onboarding, no public self-serve signup
notes: Docs are public but credentials issued only to paying customers.

name: Slack
notes: Free workspace + free app creation. Official remote MCP available.

name: Twilio
notes: Free trial credit; publishes an official MCP server.

name: Zoho Cliq
notes: Free tier; same Zoho OAuth pattern as Zoho CRM.

name: Lark (Larksuite)
notes: Free developer app registration.

name: Pumble
blocker: Very small/early API surface
notes: Free plan; API is minimal compared to Slack.

name: Discord
notes: Free bot/app registration via Developer Portal.

name: Telegram
notes: Bot token free via @BotFather, no approval needed.

name: WhatsApp Business
blocker: Requires Meta Business verification + phone number registration; template message approval
notes: Sandbox self-serve exists but production needs business verification.

name: Aircall
notes: Free trial provides API credentials from dashboard.

name: Vonage
notes: Free trial credit, self-serve API key immediately.

name: Google Ads
blocker: Developer token approval (basic/standard access) required beyond a test account
notes: Test accounts self-serve; production-level access token needs Google review.

name: Meta Ads
blocker: App review + Business verification for production ad-account access
notes: Sandbox/dev mode self-serve; live access is gated.

name: LinkedIn Ads
blocker: Marketing Developer Platform access requires LinkedIn partner approval
notes: Most endpoints require an approved partner program application.

name: GoHighLevel
notes: Free trial/agency account; marketplace app OAuth flow is public.

name: Mailchimp
notes: Free plan issues API key instantly.

name: Klaviyo
notes: Free plan; ships an official MCP server.

name: systeme.io
notes: Free plan; API key from account settings, small surface (contacts, tags, funnels).

name: Pinterest
blocker: Standard access self-serve; higher rate tiers need app review
notes: Free developer account with 'trial access', production scopes need review.

name: Threads (Meta)
blocker: App review required for most publishing scopes beyond your own test users
notes: Same Meta developer app review gate as WhatsApp/Meta Ads.

name: SendGrid
notes: Free tier (100 emails/day) with instant API key.

name: Shopify
notes: Free dev store; ships an official MCP server (Shopify Dev MCP).

name: WooCommerce
notes: Self-hosted; free, keys generated per WooCommerce install (needs a WP site, not a signup).

name: BigCommerce
notes: Free trial store gives API account instantly.

name: Salesforce Commerce Cloud
blocker: Requires an existing SFCC sandbox/license, no public self-serve trial
notes: Docs public, but sandbox access requires a customer/partner org.

name: Magento (Adobe Commerce)
blocker: Adobe Commerce (paid) is partner-gated; open-source Magento is self-hostable
notes: Split product: Adobe Commerce Cloud is sales-led, Magento Open Source is free/self-serve.

name: Squarespace
blocker: Commerce API requires an active paid Commerce plan
notes: Free trial site exists but ecommerce API needs a paid site plan.

name: Ecwid
notes: Free plan; public app OAuth flow.

name: Gumroad
notes: Free account, token from settings.

name: Amazon Selling Partner
blocker: Requires an approved Amazon seller account + app registration review
notes: Public docs, but real credentials require a vetted seller/developer account.

name: fanbasis
blocker: Dev docs portal is login/password-gated to onboarded merchants
notes: Confirmed live: dev-docs.fanbasis.com requires account login ('Invalid password' on unauthenticated access); public marketing site (fanbasis.com/enterprises) describes REST API + Postman collection + Node/Python/PHP/Ruby SDKs, but no public self-serve signup found.

name: DataForSEO
notes: Free trial credit at signup; publishes an official MCP server.

name: SE Ranking
blocker: API access tied to a paid subscription tier
notes: Trial available; full API needs a paid plan.

name: Ahrefs
blocker: API sold separately on metered credits, not bundled with base subscription
notes: Requires a paid Ahrefs account plus separate API credit purchase.

name: MrScraper
blocker: Small/newer vendor, limited public documentation depth
notes: Free trial credits at signup.

name: Apify
notes: Free tier with monthly credits; official MCP server for Actors.

name: Firecrawl
notes: Free tier credits at signup; official MCP server, built agent-first.

name: Bright Data
notes: Free trial credit; ships an official MCP server.

name: Sherlock
blocker: Not a hosted API - it's a local CLI/library, no vendor-managed auth or SLA
notes: No signup at all - it's an open-source script you run yourself; 'toolkit' would mean wrapping the CLI, not calling a REST API.

name: Waterfall.io
blocker: Could not locate public API docs; site is product-marketing only, appears sales-led/contact-to-access
notes: Low confidence - small/ambiguous vendor name, no public developer docs found. Flagged for manual follow-up, not verified live.

name: Clay
notes: Free trial; also exposes an MCP server for its enrichment 'claygent' workflows.

name: GitHub
notes: Free account, PAT instantly. Official GitHub MCP server.

name: Vercel
notes: Free hobby plan, token from dashboard; ships an official MCP server.

name: Netlify
notes: Free plan, PAT immediately.

name: Cloudflare
notes: Free plan; publishes several official remote MCP servers.

name: Supabase
notes: Free tier project instantly; official MCP server.

name: Neo4j
notes: Free Aura tier; also has an official MCP server for Cypher querying.

name: Snowflake
notes: Free 30-day trial credits; official MCP server (Cortex).

name: MongoDB Atlas
notes: Free M0 cluster forever; official MCP server.

name: Datadog
notes: Free 14-day trial, keys instantly.

name: Sentry
notes: Free developer tier; ships an official MCP server.

name: Notion
notes: Free personal plan; official MCP server.

name: Airtable
notes: Free plan, PAT immediately from account.

name: Linear
notes: Free tier; official MCP server.

name: Jira
notes: Free tier; official Atlassian remote MCP server (Jira + Confluence).

name: Asana
notes: Free tier; official MCP server.

name: Monday.com
notes: Free tier; official MCP server.

name: ClickUp
notes: Free plan; official MCP server.

name: Coda
notes: Free tier, token from account settings.

name: Smartsheet
notes: Free trial; access token from account, no approval needed for basic use.

name: Harvest
notes: Free trial; PAT generated instantly.

name: Stripe
notes: Free account, test-mode keys instantly; official MCP server.

name: Plaid
blocker: Free tier is Sandbox/Limited Production only; full Production needs Plaid review
notes: Sandbox keys instant on signup; scaling past a handful of live items needs approval.

name: Binance
notes: Free account, API key from settings (KYC required for full trading limits).

name: Paygent Connect
blocker: No public developer portal found; NMI-powered gateways are typically reseller/merchant-of-record gated
notes: Low confidence - could not find a public docs URL; treated as gated pending manual verification.

name: iPayX
what: Rail-agnostic FX cost/forensic audit layer for B2B payments
notes: Confirmed live: ships its own MCP server (mcp-fx-audit) with a free no-auth FX-compare tool and a paid Bearer-token audit-report tool. Already agent-native by design, not a traditional payments API.

name: QuickBooks
notes: Free developer sandbox company instantly; production keys need Intuit app review before public launch.

name: Xero
notes: Free developer account + demo company; official MCP server.

name: Brex
blocker: API access tied to an active Brex customer account (enterprise-onboarded)
notes: Docs are public but you need a real funded Brex account to get credentials.

name: Ramp
blocker: API access tied to an active Ramp customer account
notes: Has an MCP server, but both need a real funded Ramp account, same as Brex.

name: PitchBook
blocker: Public docs exist, but the API is a separate paid contract - keys are issued only after a sales conversation with your account manager
notes: CORRECTED after verification: first pass assumed 'no public docs' (wrong) - docs are public (documenter.getpostman.com, pitchbook.com/help) and the surface is broad (companies, deals, people, investors, funds). The gate is commercial (contract + credits), not documentation.

name: NotebookLM
blocker: Consumer NotebookLM has no public API; programmatic access only via Gemini Enterprise
notes: The consumer product most people know has no API at all - only the enterprise Gemini variant exposes one.

name: Otter AI
blocker: No traditional public REST docs; access is via an MCP server tied to a paid/business account
notes: MCP-only distribution model - no classic REST API to point to.

name: Fathom
blocker: No public API docs found on fathom.video; enterprise/Zapier-only integration path
notes: Low confidence - could not locate a public developer portal.

name: Consensus
what: AI search engine over peer-reviewed research papers
notes: Confirmed live: official MCP server at mcp.consensus.app with OAuth, no account needed for basic use, free account for more. Separate raw REST API is 'available by application only' with per-call pricing - MCP is the self-serve path, REST is gated.

name: Reducto
notes: Confirmed live: REST API with Bearer API key, CLI, and an official MCP server for agent tool-calling; credit-based self-serve signup.

name: Devin
notes: Confirmed live: first-party MCP server at mcp.devin.ai using Bearer API key auth (org-scoped service-user keys), same auth model as the REST API.

name: higgsfield
blocker: CLI-based content suite, no public REST docs found, credits/waitlist model
notes: Low confidence - primarily a consumer app with a CLI; no clear self-serve developer API found.

name: Mermaid CLI
blocker: Not a hosted API - local CLI/npm package, no vendor auth or account at all
notes: Free and open, but 'toolkit' means wrapping a local binary, not calling a hosted service.

name: YouTube Transcript
notes: Free tier with API key from signup; unofficial third-party wrapper around YouTube's caption data, not a Google/YouTube product.

name: Grain
blocker: API access tied to paid Business/Enterprise plan
notes: Public marketing site references integrations/API, but full docs sit behind a paid plan.
